SMALL CLAIMS CALENDAR OF JAN-12-2021 - TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION AS OF JAN-12-2021 BY PANG LY. PLAINTIFF PAUL A FRASSETTO IS PRESENT. DEFENDANT STEVEN BRUCE IS NOT PRESENT. DEFENDANT PEOPLE WITH DISABILITIES FUNDATION IS NOT PRESENT. NO EXHIBITS RETAINED. COMMISSIONER: PANG LY; CLERK: R. SAHATJIAN. NOT REPORTED.(D506)

MOTION TO VACATE JUDGMENT FILED BY STEVEN BRUCE, IS GRANTED. COURT DISCLOSURE REGARDING CAMPAIGN CONTRIBUTIONS IS MADE. THE COURT FINDS THAT PLAINTIFF'S POS WAS PROPERLY SERVED, EVEN THOUGH HE DID NOT FILE THE POS WITH THE COURT PRIOR TO THE HEARING THAT OCCURRED ON JANUARY 12, 2021. THE COURT FURTHER FINDS THAT JURISDICTION WAS PROPER AND AWARDED $6797.47 AND COSTS TO THE PLAINTIFF. PLAINTIFF PAUL A FRASSETTO IS PRESENT. DEFENDANTS PEOPLE WITH DISABILITIES FOUNDATION, REPRESENTED BY STEPHEN BRUCE AND STEVEN BRUCE ARE PRESENT. PLAINTIFF AND DEFENDANT EXHIBITS ARE REVIEWED AND RETURNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MICHELLE TONG; CLERK: W. TRUPEK. NOT REPORTED.(506)

SMALL CLAIMS APPEAL CALENDAR OF MAY-12-2021 - JUDGMENT ENTERED IN FAVOR OF PLAINTIFF/RESPONDENT PAUL A. FRASSETTO AND AGAINST STEVEN BRUCE AND PEOPLE WITH DISABILITIES FOUNDATION, JOINTLY AND SEVERALLY, IN THE AMOUNT OF $6,822.47. JUDGE: M. WISS, CLERK: R. GUMPAL, NOT REPORTED, (305).

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT RESPONDENT FRASSETTO, PAUL A RECOVER FROM APPELLANT BRUCE, STEVEN PEOPLE WITH DISABILITIES FOUNDATION $6,747.47 PRINCIPAL, $75.00 FILING FEE, TOTAL JUDGMENT $6,822.47, THIS JUDGMENT ON APPEAL IS FINAL AND REPLACES THE SMALL CLAIMS JUDGMENT OF MARCH 17, 2021. EXHIBITS ARE ORDERED PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECION 1952. SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT FOR DETAILS. calendar.matters:
SMALL CLAIMS APPEAL NOTICE OF TIME AND PLACE
Off Calendar May-12-2021 Set For Trial On May-12-21 At 10:00 A.M. In Dept 305

SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar Jun-09-2021 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: Appearances on WED-MAY-12-2021:
Paul A. Frasetto, Plaintiff / Respondent appearing in Propria Persona.
Steven Bruce, Defendant / Appellant appearing in Propria Persona and as a representative on behalf of Defendant / Appellant People with Disabilities Foundation.

This cause comes on calendar, as assigned by the Presiding Judge, for trial de novo. Both parties are sworn.

Due to a discrepancy in the court's record, Mr. Bruce is instructed to file a declaration with the court to correct his address and telephone number.

Prior to trial, Mr. Bruce indicates Judge Wiss was the settlement conference judge in the underlying case of Baker vs. People with Disabilities Foundation, Case No. CGC-14-537885. Both parties indicate they have no objection to the Court trying this case. The Court also inquires of the fee arbitration.

The Court notes the March 23, 2021 Appeal is filed on behalf of People with Disabilities Foundation only and not on behalf of Steven Bruce. The Court questions the jurisdiction.

Paul A. Fressetto testifies in the narrative on his own behalf. The following documentary exhibits are introduced and identified:

Exhibit B - Complaint for Breach of Written Contract (Baker v. PWDF)
Exhibit C - Amended Cross-Complaint (Baker v. PWDF)
Exhibit D - Judgment on Jury Verdict (Baker v. PWDF)
Exhibit E - Amended Order Granting in Part and Denying in Part Motion for Attorney's Fees (Baker v. PWDF)
Exhibit F - Demand for Arbitration
Exhibit G - Counter Claim
Exhibit H - Stipulation for Settlement
Exhibit I - Cost Bill
Exhibit L - Notice of Client's Right to Fee Arbitration (People with Disabilities Foundation)
Exhibit K - Notice of Client's Right to Fee Arbitration (Steven Bruce)
Exhibit N - Summary of Damages

Steven Bruce testifies in the narrative on his own behalf. The following documentary exhibits are introduced and identified:

Exhibit 1 - Beginning of Deposition of Diliberto
Exhibit 7 - Managing Attorney Zoya Yarnykh's Letter
Exhibit 2 - Frasetto Mediation Brief

The Court questions Mr. Bruce re the Notice of Appeal filed on March 23, 2021. Copies of the Appeal are provided to both parties. The appeal is filed on behalf of the People with Disabilities Foundation. Mr. Bruce resumes testifying in the narrative.

Mr. Fraseetto presents rebuttal argument. The following documentary exhibit is introduced and identified:

Exhibit A - Retainer Agreement Contingent

At the conclusion of trial, the matter is taken under submission. Plaintiff's and Defendant's exhibits are retained by the court.

Judge: Hon. Mary E. Wiss; Clerk: L. Fong; Proceedings Not Reported; Dept. 305.
